value: Odysseus is the central brain/orchestrator of the workspace; reads repos, schedules tasks, kicks off agents, monitors containers, and routes work rather than coding everything itself
Odysseus connects via a shared drive mount (every repo is a file path away) and the Docker socket (restart containers, trigger builds, kick off agent loops)
Odysseus runs locally at http://localhost:7000 with a REST API; Bearer token auth via tokens generated in Settings → API Tokens, stored as ODYSSEUS_URL and ODYSSEUS_TOKEN env vars
Self-referential core workspace; writes its own skills/docs/config and can restart itself via Docker socket; connects via /api/codex/* + Docker socket + shared drive; status running, ongoing priority

Multi-service AI ops platform; sister platform to Odysseus sharing Ollama at :8081; containers include motor-pool app, dashboard :3000, agent-db Postgres :5432, nemoclaw :9000, tool-content-gen :3200, and tool-website :3201; high priority

Personal knowledge vault and Obsidian vault; Odysseus's primary external brain; holds Obsidian notes, 15 years of enterprise automation scripts, and MD runbooks/decisions; git-tracked and Obsidian-readable; high priority

Meta-repo intelligence layer that aggregates what needs to happen next across all repos (stalled PRs, vuln fixes, missing docs, ROADMAP/TASKS/FEATURES health); reads workspace MD files directly and can take or delegate action; high priority
FIRE tracker and API server; Odysseus polls periodically and surfaces numbers in a morning briefing; financial context, low/eventual priority

VHS collection index, active dev for girlfriend; Odysseus can curate entries, research valuations, and write back to JSON via shell; one of only 3 repos with a NeonDB; low-medium priority, revisit when active
K4 cipher toolkit side project; has a NeonDB for cipher analysis state; cool autonomous sub-agent pattern but low priority
Blackboard MCP server, part of motor-pool; deprioritized for now; will eventually demonstrate a semi-complex MCP layered on a local/remote LLM inside motor-pool
Generative AI model trainer; deprioritized; potential future motor-pool experience (user uploads images, motor-pool runs fine-tune pipeline)
Chrome job application autofill tool; explicitly dropped/lowered priority
Google Drive audit/copy CLI utility; role TBD, basic Gemini sufficient for now
Forked video editor being explored; potential future motor-pool experience as its own SLM/container for editing AI-generated content
darkmoon (multiplayer 3D game) and games (browser arcade) are live proofs of concept tracked only for CI failure/downtime alerts; farm-3j is a client PoC tracked on downtime
skyview (client drone site) and osrs (OSRS bot) are intentionally ignored with no Odysseus integration
Only overseer, vhs, and kryptos have NeonDB instances; overseer's holds repo health scores, vhs holds collection data, kryptos holds cipher analysis state
Unknown role container in motor-pool (nemoclaw:latest, port 9000→8080); needs investigation before being wired into automated flows

key: Known soft spots in the system
value: Four flagged gaps: nemoclaw's role is unclear and needs investigation; Docker socket reliability is unproven and needs hardened testing before self-restart is trusted; stash lacks clear write/naming/commit conventions; the overseer loop (audit to todos to action to rescore) is not yet closed end to end

Bearer tokens carry scopes per domain: todos:read/write, calendar:read/write, documents:read/write, memory:read/write, email:read + email:draft/send, cookbook:read/launch, and chat; GET /api/codex/capabilities shows what a given token can actually do

Agent-safe endpoints live under /api/codex/* (todos, calendar/events, documents, memory) plus /api/chat and /api/chat_stream for sessions, /api/tasks for the scheduler, and /api/webhooks/{event} for event triggers; admin-only routes like /api/mcp/servers require a cookie session, not a Bearer token

POST /api/tasks supports schedule types once, daily, weekly, monthly, cron (full cron expression), and event (fires on internal events like session_created); used today for things like a daily morning-briefing LLM prompt

Planned rollout order: 1) stash session-dump task (zero setup), 2) GitHub MCP server for PRs/CI/issues, 3) overseer workspace read + weekly ops cron, 4) Docker socket health monitor, 5) motor-pool tool webhooks, 6) fire API polling, 7) VHS + NeonDB, 8) opencut-classic evaluation
Goal is for stash to become a permanent git-tracked Obsidian-readable record of everything the workspace does, auto-fed via session dumps, research outputs, decisions, and generated skills

Goal is a Sunday 9am cron where Odysseus reads ROADMAP/TASKS/FEATURES across all repos, checks GitHub via MCP, scores repo health, creates todos, and writes a synthesized weekly priorities doc to stash/ops
Longer-term goal is for Odysseus to kick off Claude Code or codex agent containers as sub-tasks once Docker socket reliability is proven out

ARGUS.md is the living, aspirational improvement checklist for Odysseus across 11 areas (MCPs, Obsidian, chat, research, email/calendar, memory, comparison tools, cookbook, gallery, notes/tasks, UI); intended as a monthly-revisit source of truth, almost entirely unchecked/not yet built
Audit all current MCPs in the registry, verify each is running, document failed states with repair steps, and set up alerts for future MCP failures

Confirm the Obsidian vault path is configured (config/obsidian.yml), enable automatic two-way sync between Obsidian and Odysseus, scaffold a sample vault folder structure, and write a macro to import a daily note into the Odysseus brain
Add persona prompt templates (dev_bot, researcher, assistant) as a reusable chat widget, store best-practice prompts in _knowledgebase/chat-personas.md, and add a Smart Reply UI toggle
Build a template for comprehensive research reports, hook research outputs into the comparison tool, automate periodic research on a chosen topic, and export reports to PDF stored in Obsidian
Create a mailbox parser for auto-classification, schedule a daily email digest via manage_tasks, link calendar reminders to manage_notes checklist items, and add a meeting follow-up note template
Log key decisions in a memos/ folder, build a Lessons Learned knowledge base, and set up a neural-network-driven suggestion engine for next steps
Integrate a compare view into the dashboard, write unit tests for the comparison algorithm, and add a visual diff inside Obsidian
Add a qwen3-8b 4-bit preset, write a walkthrough for running it, and publish the preset to the shared Cookbook repo
Import recent screenshots into the gallery, tag items (design, documentation), and link library artifacts to related Odysseus tasks
Use ARGUS.md as a baseline checklist alongside Tasks/Notes, automate task creation from email requests, and sync unfinished tasks to Obsidian daily
Build or adapt a dark-mode theme matching brand guidelines, and add an accessibility layer (high-contrast mode, font size options)
Prefers Odysseus to act as a thin orchestrator/router rather than doing heavy coding itself, delegating execution to motor-pool and other repos
Prefers using plain MD files (ROADMAP/TASKS/FEATURES) as the interface between repos and the orchestrator rather than building bespoke APIs first
Prefers flagging unknowns (e.g. nemoclaw, Docker socket reliability) explicitly as soft spots to investigate before wiring them into automated/autonomous flows

Wants the agent to clearly distinguish what is actually built/working (e.g. the live /api/codex/* endpoints) from what is aspirational/planned (e.g. most of the ARGUS.md checklist and ecosystem map integrations); most of the ecosystem map and ARGUS checklist describe goals to work towards, not current state
Personal multi-repo AI ops ecosystem centered on Odysseus as orchestrator, with motor-pool, stash, and overseer as the three high-priority pillars and a long tail of lower-priority/experimental repos
Multi-container stack (dashboard, content-gen tool, website tool, nemoclaw, shared Ollama, Postgres) that Odysseus monitors and can delegate heavy agent tasks to

Active project tracked in ARGUS.md: an 11-area, mostly-unbuilt improvement checklist for Odysseus (MCPs, Obsidian sync, chat personas, deep research, email/calendar automation, memory system, comparison tools, cookbook presets, gallery, notes/tasks, UI theming), meant to be revisited monthly
